engineering_defaults_locked_now.behavior: Users outside the memory cohort keep current legacy /v3 behavior as the selected primary path.
Enrolled users with missing, malformed, uid-mismatched, unsupported-schema, or timed-out control state fail closed before legacy reads.
Treat absent/revoked default-memory grant as privacy/consent denial unless product explicitly separates rollout from consent.
When memory compatibility projection is enabled and empty, return [] and do not query legacy afterward.
Do not add include_archive=true or Archive vector behavior to the initial /v3 cutover.
Keep the JSON body MemoryDB-compatible; expose route-level read-source/read-decision/next-cursor diagnostics through additive headers.
Add opaque HMAC keyset cursor mode for memory canaries while preserving offset behavior only for legacy-primary clients.
Do not merge legacy and memory result pages and do not treat None/missing/exceptions as permission to use legacy.

irreducible_product_api_decisions.escalation: Escalate only if product states default-memory grant is merely rollout eligibility; then add a separate consent control.
Decide how long old clients retain offset/first-page behavior; do not reproduce the 5000-item override in memory cursor mode.

oracle_prescriptive_recommendations.model_selection_caveat: gpt-5.5-pro browser returned guidance; model-selection evidence reported resolved unavailable/verified no.
oracle_prescriptive_recommendations.summary: Oracle recommends preserving /v3's default List[MemoryDB] body while adding a memory compatibility adapter over memory-authoritative writes and a memory-derived users/{uid}/memories projection. Non-enrolled users stay legacy-primary; enrolled malformed/missing/control-timeout states fail closed; no-grant denies; enabled-empty returns [] without legacy fallback; Archive is not launched on default /v3; cursor mode is additive, signed, keyset, and generation/projection bound.

product_decision_dependencies.needed_decision: Whether external /v3 callers should receive explicit denial, empty safe response, or opt-in legacy-safe behavior for disabled/malformed/missing/no-grant state.
Confirm enabled-empty memory memory_items returns [] and must not fall back to stale legacy memories.
Define additive external source metadata and defaulted category/review/manual/edit provenance semantics for MemoryDB-compatible clients.
Define stable cursor pagination semantics or explicitly retain legacy offset semantics for /v3 cutover.
Decide whether /v3 writes are dual-written/converged to memory before reads can use memory by default.

remaining_gaps.required_proof: Decide and test whether disabled, malformed, missing, or no-grant rollout returns legacy-safe, empty, or explicit denial for `/v3`; do not allow implicit unsafe legacy fallback after memory-write states.
When memory default reads are explicitly enabled and `memory_items` is empty, prove `/v3` returns an empty memory result without falling back to stale legacy rows.
Define and test additive memory/default policy and source metadata in the external MemoryDB-compatible response shape without silently fabricating category/review/manual/edit fields.
Prove Archive tier is default-unavailable for `/v3` unless an explicit Archive-capable product decision exists.
`/v3` currently exposes limit/offset only; category/filter compatibility needs a product/API decision and fixtures before runtime changes.
External create/edit/delete must have a durable memory convergence plan before `/v3` read fallback semantics can be broadened.
Current `/v3` uses limit/offset with a first-page limit override; stable memory cursor pagination remains unproven.

memory cursor mode marker proving offset and the legacy 5000 first-page override were not applied.

proposed_safe_cutover_sequence.description: Add the real server-side memory cohort/control/grant reader behind bounded fail-closed semantics.
Add the real memory-derived compatibility projection read API/store with generation/freshness checks.
Prove external /v3 create/update/delete converge to memory authoritative state before reads use memory.
Configure real cursor signing secret and integrate route validation for memory cursor mode only.
Extend real-router TestClient proofs to actual auth/rate-limit/dependency behavior for GET.
Only after gates pass, make the default-off GET seam effectively route real traffic through memory.
Run shadow/canary with telemetry for read-source/decision/failure/cursor/projection-generation and rollback.
Require explicit product/privacy/operational approval before expanding beyond canary.

remaining_gates.required_evidence: Real memory cohort/enrollment/control read source must be server-side, bounded, schema/uid validated, and fail-closed for enrolled missing/malformed/timeout states, without client-side direct control reads if that is unsafe.

Future GET must read expected_account_generation from the independent server-owned users/{uid}/memory_state/head source, then require trusted == control == projection == cursor generation; local writer/emulator evidence now exists, but real-service runtime gates are still required before effective memory read behavior.

Real memory-derived compatibility projection read API/store must return MemoryDB-compatible items, prove ready empty projection state returns [], and expose generation/freshness fences without legacy fallback.

Real external write-convergence/source-of-truth evidence for /v3 create/update/delete must prove memory authoritative writes, projection commits, tombstones, vector cleanup fences, and no direct legacy write fallback before memory read cutover.
Real cursor signing secret/config and route validation integration must reject tamper/expiry/filter or generation mismatch and must not apply offset=0 -> limit=5000 behavior in memory cursor mode.

Real route-level dependency overrides/auth/rate-limit behavior under TestClient must execute GET through the actual router dependencies while keeping production app startup and mutating routes out of scope.
Backward compatibility for non-enrolled legacy users must preserve current limit/offset semantics, including offset=0 -> limit=5000 first-page behavior only on the legacy-primary path.

Enrolled fail-closed/no-grant/projection-not-ready/write-not-ready behavior must return the prescribed deny/fail-closed response with no legacy fallback, no memory/legacy merge, and no exception downgrade.
Archive default-unavailable and stale Short-term not default-visible proof must be preserved in real route fixtures, projection reads, cursor mode, and observability before exposing memory GET reads.

Observability/telemetry gates and explicit approval gates must exist for read source, decision, failure reason, projection generation, cursor validation, canary cohort, rollback, and product/privacy approval.
